["point 1", "point 2", ...],"aiConcepts": ["AI concept 1", "AI concept 2", ...],"technicalDetails": ["tool/framework 1", "implementation detail", ...],"visualContextFlags": [{"timestamp": 120, "reason": "Code demo", "context": "Shows Python implementation"}]}If the video is not about AI/tech, set aiConcepts and technicalDetails to empty arrays but still extract keyPoints.TRANSCRIPT:all righty guys welcome back to another AI news Roundup we've got so much to talk about today as usual uh brand new open source models have dropped as well as closed Source models from Big competitors we've also got new products research and demos for video generation that are just mindblowing so plenty to discuss all great things happening in the AI space there's a ton of competition right now and it really feels like we're kind of at that Hard launch takeoff moment we knew the cat was out of the bag when chat GPT released but man I don't think people are ready for what this cat is capable of doing all right enough talk let's dive in open ai's brand new deep research model the highest benching model open AI has ever put out there for us has already been entirely jailbroken by Elder pus AKA plyy The Liberator plyy is known throughout the web for jailbreaking AI models and releasing those jailbreaks publicly on his GitHub he's the best of the best and even just mentioning his jailbreaks and his name to deep seek R1 could result in the model becoming jailbroken so it's it's no shock that he is jailbroken open ai's deep research but it does beg the question at least in my mind why are we having all of these high safety tuned closed Source AI models if they can all just be jailbroken anyways without really too many fancy tricks like literally I could just go to GitHub download some of the jailbreaks they're literally just prompts and put them through the model and I end up with a jail broken model so anyways he got it to do a PhD level drug synthesis recipes how to get away with unal living people making poison uh yeah it it it'll do anything so jailbroken means jailbroken diving super deep on this topic isn't in the scope of this video but I'm sure you guys in the comments as always will provide lots of awesome Insight lots of great takes and opinions and we can have fruitful discussion down there if there's one thing that always impresses me about you guys is it's your ability to think outside the box communicate with each other and share ideas and we need more of that especially if we are in a hard takeoff AI scenario and speaking of open- source models there is a deep research competitor by hugging face that is fully open source and you know comes out within 4 days I think of open ai's deep research and they also did this in only 24 hours a 24-hour deadline to repli replicate and open source deep research uh yeah so this agent can obviously navigate the web autonomously scroll and search through pages download and manipulate files run calculations on data they aimed for the best performance but I think they did a pretty great job at matching up to deep research with that 24-hour deadline absolutely crazy yeah so on the Gaia Benchmark deep research from open AI gets a 67% accuracy which is great deep research falls behind it at 55% but it's fully open source it can be modified it can be made better to get better scores on benchmarks and I'll bet bet you it doesn't cost 200 bucks a month to get access to this model and again it brings up the question it's like we have these closed Source models from the likes of open AI or Google but then they just get replicated by the open source field anyways in very quickly at break neck pacing so not only do the closed Source models get jailbroken anyways but they also get replaced by open- Source models that don't need to be jailbroken in the first place in a lot of circumstances again you guys can debate healthily and the comments about whether or not this is a good or bad thing all I can really say is the speed of AI development is just rapid it's blistering unlike anything I've ever seen in my quite young life all right and other hugging face related news they actually updated their website as well it now has an AI based search function that allows you to pretty much just ask it what you are trying to do give it your specific context your specific scenario and it will access the hugging face space database of over 400,000 spaces to find exactly what you need to get the job done a nice quick little demo video here brought To Us by Victor mustar you can generate a 3D model from an image it will pull up the best spaces for that particular task or you know image to video generation whatever it might be so this is actually a really cool feature that I have wanted on hugging
